name: Collado de Comiols y Roc de Benavent — circular por Roca Roja
region_intro: Hiking around Isona i Conca Dellà reveals a landscape shaped by the Pre-Pyrenees, featuring a mix of rolling hills, river valleys, and forested areas. The region is characterized by its geological formations, including limestone outcrops and ancient riverbeds, providing varied terrain for exploration. Trails often traverse agricultural lands, connect small villages, and offer views of the surrounding mountainous backdrop. Best hiking trails around Isona i Conca Dellà The most popular hiking route is Sant Pere de Vilanoveta from Aramunt — circular through the Bosque de Sant Corneli, a 10.6 miles (17.1 km) trail that takes about 6 hours to complete. This difficult route explores dense woodlands and offers significant elevation changes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Salàs de Pallars Loop, an easy 2.7 miles (4.3 km) path. This shorter trail provides a gentle walk through the local countryside. Local hikers also love the Nerets Loop, a 6.4 miles (10.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain including forested sections and open views, often completed in about 3 hours 25 minutes. Hiking around Isona i Conca Dellà is defined by its mix of rolling hills, river valleys, and dense woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy hikes to more challenging routes. The routes in Isona i Conca Dellà are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Isona i Conca Dellà's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Isona i Conca Dellà?
A: There are over 70 hiking routes recorded in Isona i Conca Dellà, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. This includes 17 easy, 41 moderate, and 20 difficult trails.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Isona i Conca Dellà offers several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1219755152/salas-de-pallars-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salàs de Pallars Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 2.7-mile (4.3 km) path that provides a gentle walk through the local countryside.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of hikes in Isona i Conca Dellà?
A: Hikes in the region vary significantly. You can find shorter, easy walks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1219755152/salas-de-pallars-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salàs de Pallars Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 2.7 miles (4.3 km) and takes around 1 hour 15 minutes. More challenging routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1220076476/sant-pere-de-vilanoveta-from-aramunt-circular-through-the-bosque-de-sant-corneli" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Pere de Vilanoveta from Aramunt&lt;/a&gt;, can be over 10 miles (17 km) and require up to 6 hours to complete.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Isona i Conca Dellà?
A: The best time for hiking in Isona i Conca Dellà is generally during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is mild, and the landscapes are particularly vibrant. Summer can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter offers opportunities for quieter walks, though some higher trails might be affected by snow.

Q: What kind of natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the hiking trails?
A: The region is rich in geological formations and historical sites. You can explore impressive canyons like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5269223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegats Gorge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/323889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roca Regina and Barranc del Bosc&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, several trails pass by ancient castles, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1659273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Mur&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5926288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Claverol Castle&lt;/a&gt;, offering a glimpse into the area's history.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Isona i Conca Dellà?
A: Yes, many routes in Isona i Conca Dellà are designed as circular walks, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1219734884/nerets-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nerets Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular trail, covering about 6.4 miles (10.4 km) through varied terrain.

Q: Is parking available at trailheads in Isona i Conca Dellà?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many villages and popular starting points for trails offer designated parking areas. It's advisable to check specific route descriptions on komoot for details on parking facilities near your chosen hike.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Isona i Conca Dellà?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in Isona i Conca Dellà. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife, and to follow local regulations regarding pet access.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Isona i Conca Dellà?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's diverse landscape, which includes rolling hills, river valleys, and dense woodlands, as well as the geological formations and historical points of interest along the routes.

Q: Are there any trails that offer significant elevation changes for a challenging hike?
A: For those seeking a challenge, trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1220076476/sant-pere-de-vilanoveta-from-aramunt-circular-through-the-bosque-de-sant-corneli" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Pere de Vilanoveta from Aramunt&lt;/a&gt; are ideal. This difficult route features significant elevation changes, with over 900 meters of ascent, exploring dense woodlands and offering a strenuous but rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any trails that are suitable for both hiking and cycling?
A: While many trails are primarily for hiking, some routes may be suitable for both activities, depending on the terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1219706553/geobike-xl-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Geobike XL Route&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is an easy path that can be enjoyed by hikers and cyclists alike, offering a different way to experience the landscape.
tips: Coll de Comiols is a hill at 1,100.8 meters above the limits of the municipalities of Artesa de Segre, La Noguera, and Isona and Conca Dellà (formerly the Benavent district of Tremp), in Pallars Jussà
La Roca Roja is a mountain of 1,239 meters that is located just above the town of Benavent de la Conca and that gives impressive views of the Conca de Tremp
There is a viewpoint that is located at the edge of the cliff and that provides a space of tranquility enjoying one of the best views in the area.
Small town located below the Coll de Comiols and at the foot of the Roc de Benavent, a stone structure that can be reached on foot from the town itself.
